sourceMovie.title: Silent Rage
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Silent Rage works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Apocalypse
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Silent Rage clicked for you, Resident Evil: Apocalypse (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Blade
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Silent Rage clicked for you, Blade (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Samaritan
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Silent Rage clicked for you, Samaritan (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Silent Rage?
faq.answer: Try Resident Evil: Apocalypse, Blade, Samaritan—each shares DNA with Silent Rage while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Silent Rage?
faq.answer: Silent Rage (1982) is best described as Action, Horror, Science Fiction, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Silent Rage worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
